hero_tpv.scale_note: at $1M/day TPV → ~938,367x tonight's pace (a ratio, not a load test — this pipeline has not been run at that volume)
hero_tpv.method_note: Tonight's fleet ran at a ~$1.07/day pace over 81 txns / 16 agents; scale multiple = $1M/day / that rate.

hero_capture_ratio.subline: holds are ~5.6x oversized vs settlement — a float inefficiency, not lost revenue (authorize $1.00 → capture $0.18)
hero_capture_ratio.scale_note: instantaneously frozen ≈ $61–$138 at $1M/day TPV (Little's Law; holds clear in 5.3–12.0s) — lever = hold-lifetime & max_tokens right-sizing. Assumes steady-state (non-bursty) call arrivals — see dryrun/float_model.md §5.
hero_capture_ratio.scope_note: Scope: n=27 LLM chains (sapiom_openrouter only), one session, gpt-4o-mini — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.
hero_capture_ratio.method_note: Sigma settled (0.004998) / Sigma held (0.027838) dollar-weighted across all 27 supersession chains (hold → final capture). Little's Law: frozen$ = held$/day × (hold_lifetime_sec / 86400) — at $1M/day TPV, p50 (5.29s) → $61.28, p95 (11.96s) → $138.44. Full derivation + sensitivity: dryrun/float_model.md. (Superseded framing: naively scaling the capture ratio gives $4,569,828 — a per-day FLOW, not an instantaneous stock; it implicitly assumes a ~4.57-day hold lifetime vs. the measured 5.3-12.0s, ~33,000x off. See float_model.md §4d.)

hero_reconciliation.subline: naive sum overstates +10% — must filter, not sum, chains
hero_reconciliation.scale_note: at $1M/day TPV → ~$100K/day phantom spend if uncorrected (assumes tonight's traffic mix/restatement rate holds at scale)
hero_reconciliation.ingest_note: Ties out as of this ledger snapshot (ingested through 2026-07-04 11:06:35.634000). The live account has since spent more in dry-run experiments not captured in this snapshot (see RUN_LOG.md) — re-ingest before claiming this ties out today.
hero_reconciliation.method_note: Latest balance snapshot vs (initial balance − live spend): diff $0.000000. Naive = every cost row including superseded holds ($0.305310); live = superseded_at IS NULL only ($0.277472).

tile_auth_to_capture.footnote: Flat single-row services show a small negative latency — the cost row is written as part of authorization itself, before authorizedAt is stamped, not a bug. Only chained/restated services (LLM calls) show genuine positive wait for final capture.

tile_take_rate.note: 9-service sweep (dryrun/service_sweep_result.json), N=1 call per service, full table + MED/DROP rows + sources in take_rate.md. No blended headline is shown: the two real-dollar-volume services (search, images) settle at exactly vendor list price (0% markup); the two tiny-dollar rows (llm, audio) are near-certain minimum-billing-floor artifacts, not percentage markups. Of the dollar-weighted margin across all 4 rows, audio/ElevenLabs is ~88% and llm/OpenRouter is ~12% (recomputed from this same table — corrects take_rate.md's/NARRATIVE.md's earlier, backwards claim that the LLM row drove 'almost entirely' of the margin).

tile_loss_rate.note: 2/81 txns failed (2.5%) but 0/2 produced a cost row — Sapiom did not charge for either failure in this sample (both were pre-settlement client/gateway errors, not mid-flight failures after a hold). Loss rate = 0 bps of TPV. Full queries + caveats: loss_rate.md.
tile_loss_rate.cross_reference: 0 bps applies only to this n=81 sample's pre-hold failures. Post-hold failures are NOT free: see Refund-on-Failure — in a direct test, 4/4 forced post-hold failures had their hold retained/frozen (not released), not this 0 bps rate.

tile_auth_rate.note: No spending rules were active in this sample — 100% reflects an unconfigured account (nothing to deny), not proof governance works. Full distribution + caveat: findings.md §7.

tile_capital_overhang.definition: held$ ÷ settled$ across all supersession chains — same chains as Capture Ratio, inverse framing.
tile_capital_overhang.clears_note: Clears in 5.3–12.0s — not permanently parked capital.
tile_capital_overhang.scope_note: Scope: n=27 LLM chains (sapiom_openrouter only), one session, gpt-4o-mini — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.

tile_blast_radius.definition: Max spend one agent reaches before a cap stops it.
tile_blast_radius.note: Needs governance rules active — no spending rule was configured in this sample (BACKLOG #8, [HUMAN-UI]).

tile_cap_utilization.note: Needs governance rules active — no per-agent budget exists without a spending rule configured.

tile_attribution_completeness.definition: % of txns with agent, traceId, service, outcome all populated.
tile_attribution_completeness.visible_caveat: 2/81 txns carry service_name='unknown' — present, but unresolved.
tile_attribution_completeness.note: 81/81 txns have all 4 fields non-null (100%), but 2/81 carry service_name='unknown' — present, but an unresolved value, so non-null isn't always meaningfully attributed. Noted, not hidden.

tile_phantom_spend_rate.definition: Naive sum of every cost row vs. live (non-superseded) spend.
tile_phantom_spend_rate.method_note: Latest balance snapshot vs (initial balance − live spend): diff $0.000000. Naive = every cost row including superseded holds ($0.305310); live = superseded_at IS NULL only ($0.277472).

tile_hold_release_latency.definition: p50/p95 time from hold to final capture (chained services only).
tile_hold_release_latency.scope_note: Scope: sapiom_openrouter only (n=31), one session, gpt-4o-mini — LLM-specific, not platform-wide.

tile_refund_on_failure.definition: % of a hold released back vs. retained/frozen, when a call fails after the hold is placed.
tile_refund_on_failure.subline: 100% of hold retained/frozen on post-hold failure (4/4 forced trials)
tile_refund_on_failure.note: In-sample: 0/2 of the n=81 sample's natural failures ever held a cost — both died pre-hold, so there was nothing to release (loss_rate.md). Direct test: when a hold DOES exist and the call then errors, 4/4 forced trials show the hold RETAINED/FROZEN — availableBalance dropped by exactly $0.076803 each time (zero variance) while totalBalance never moved, so this is not a completed charge, and it is not released either (findings.md §9; dryrun/failure_capture_n3.md; dryrun/hold_linearity_extension.md; dryrun/refund_watch.log — still being watched for a delayed release). Over-requested max_tokens makes the frozen amount larger. Honest caveat: the per-failure retention mechanic is deterministic and measured (4/4) — the FLEET FREQUENCY of post-hold failures in live traffic is NOT measured; do not read this as a $/day loss rate.

kya_scorecard.visible_caveat: Illustrative — velocity-only, one session; spend is shown but not a factor in this grade.
kya_scorecard.formula_note: Velocity score = 60 pts if peer-relative velocity anomaly flagged (findings.md §5) + up to 30 pts scaled from peak calls in any 60s window (peak x 3, capped). Grade: A 0-9 / B 10-24 / C 25-49 / D 50-74 / F 75-100. Agents with <3 calls show N/A — not enough data for a median gap. Spend is NOT part of this score (spend-runaway is ~54% of all TPV and grades C; fleet-test is ~0.4% of TPV and grades F) — velocity-only, illustrative, one session.
